001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.markdown;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022import java.util.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.commons.function.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.*;
028import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json5.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.marshall.swap.*;
032
033/**
034 * Session object for {@link MarkdownParser}.
035 *
036 * <p>
037 * Parses Markdown tables and bulleted lists into Java objects.
038 *
039 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
040 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not thread safe and is typically discarded after one use.
041 * </ul>
042 *
043 */
044@SuppressWarnings({
045   "java:S110", // Inheritance depth acceptable for parser session hierarchy
046   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
047   "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for doParse / parseAnything
048   "java:S6541", // Brain method acceptable for parseAnything
049   "unchecked",
050   "rawtypes",
051   "resource" // Closeable resources are owned by the caller's parser session; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
052})
053public class MarkdownParserSession extends ReaderParserSession implements RecordReadable {
054
055   private static final String CONST_type = "_type";
056
057   final String nullValue;
058   private final Memoizer<JsonParser> json5Parser = memoizer(() -> {
059      var b = Json5Parser.create().marshallingContext((MarshallingContext) getContext());
060      if (isTrimStrings())
061         b = b.trimStrings();
062      return b.build();
063   });
064
065   /**
066    * Builder class.
067    */
068   @SuppressWarnings({
069      "java:S119" // 'SELF' (CRTP self-type) is intentional and clearer than a single-letter name.
070   })
071   public abstract static class Builder<SELF extends Builder<SELF>> extends ReaderParserSession.Builder<SELF> {
072
073      String nullValue;
074
075      /**
076       * Constructor.
077       *
078       * @param ctx The context creating this session.
079       */
080      protected Builder(MarkdownParser ctx) {
081         super(ctx);
082         nullValue = ctx.getNullValue();
083      }
084
085      @Override
086      public MarkdownParserSession build() {
087         return new MarkdownParserSession(this);
088      }
089
090   }
091
092   /**
093    * Concrete default builder leaf for the non-subclassed {@code create()} path (CRTP terminal).
094    */
095   public static final class DefaultBuilder extends Builder<DefaultBuilder> {
096
097      DefaultBuilder(MarkdownParser ctx) {
098         super(ctx);
099      }
100   }
101
102   /**
103    * Creates a new builder for this object.
104    *
105    * @param ctx The context creating this session.
106    * @return A new builder.
107    */
108   @SuppressWarnings({
109      "java:S1452" // Builder<?> wildcard return intentional; callers use it to construct session instances polymorphically
110   })
111   public static Builder<?> create(MarkdownParser ctx) {
112      return new DefaultBuilder(ctx);
113   }
114
115   /**
116    * Constructor.
117    *
118    * @param builder The builder for this object.
119    */
120   protected MarkdownParserSession(Builder<?> builder) {
121      super(builder);
122      nullValue = builder.nullValue != null ? builder.nullValue : "*null*";
123   }
124
125   @Override /* RecordReadable */
126   public RecordReader parseRecords(Object input) throws IOException {
127      return RecordAdapter.reader(this, input);
128   }
129
130   @Override /* RecordReadable */
131   public boolean isRecordStreaming() {
132      return false;
133   }
134
135   @Override /* Overridden from ParserSession */
136   protected <T> T doParse(ParserPipe pipe, ClassMeta<T> type) throws IOException, ParseException {
137      try (var r = new java.io.BufferedReader(pipe.getReader())) {
138         var lines = readAllLines(r);
139         return parseAnything(lines, type, getOuter(), null);
140      }
141   }
142
143   /**
144    * Main parse dispatch method.
145    *
146    * @param <T> The target type.
147    * @param lines All input lines.
148    * @param eType The expected type.
149    * @param outer The outer object (for inner class instantiation).
150    * @param pMeta The parent bean property meta (may be null).
151    * @return The parsed object.
152    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
153    * @throws ParseException If parsing fails.
154    */
155   protected <T> T parseAnything(List<String> lines, ClassMeta<T> eType, Object outer, BeanPropertyMeta pMeta) throws IOException, ParseException {
156      if (eType == null)
157         eType = (ClassMeta<T>) object();
158
159      var swap = (ObjectSwap<T,Object>) eType.getSwap(this);
160      var builder = (BuilderSwap<T,Object>) eType.getBuilderSwap(this);
161      ClassMeta<?> sType;
162      if (builder != null)
163         sType = builder.getBuilderClassMeta(this);
164      else if (swap != null)
165         sType = swap.getSwapClassMeta(this);
166      else
167         sType = eType;
168
169      if (sType.isOptional())
170         return (T) opt(parseAnything(lines, eType.getElementType(), outer, pMeta));
171
172      // Skip blank lines and handle empty input
173      var nonBlank = lines.stream().filter(l -> !l.isBlank()).toList();
174      if (lines.isEmpty() || nonBlank.isEmpty())
175         return sType.isString() ? (T) "" : null;
176
177      var firstNonBlank = nonBlank.get(0).trim();
178
179      Object o;
180
181      if (firstNonBlank.startsWith("|")) {
182         // Table input
183         o = parseTable(lines, sType, outer);
184      } else if (firstNonBlank.startsWith("- ") || firstNonBlank.startsWith("* ") || firstNonBlank.startsWith("+ ")) {
185         // Bulleted list input
186         o = parseBulletList(lines, sType, outer);
187      } else {
188         // Plain text — treat as simple value
189         o = parseCellValue(firstNonBlank, sType, null);
190      }
191
192      if (builder != null && o != null)
193         o = builder.build(this, o, eType);
194
195      if (swap != null && o != null)
196         o = unswap(swap, o, eType);
197
198      return (T) o;
199   }
200
201   /**
202    * Parses a Markdown table (either 2-column key/value or multi-column).
203    *
204    * @param lines All input lines.
205    * @param eType The expected type.
206    * @param outer The outer object.
207    * @return The parsed object.
208    * @throws ParseException If parsing fails.
209    */
210   protected Object parseTable(List<String> lines, ClassMeta<?> eType, Object outer) throws ParseException {
211      // Extract table rows (lines starting with |)
212      var tableLines = lines.stream().filter(l -> l.trim().startsWith("|")).toList();
213      if (tableLines.isEmpty())
214         return null;
215
216      // Parse header row
217      var headerLine = tableLines.get(0);
218      var headers = splitTableRow(headerLine);
219      if (headers.isEmpty())
220         return null;
221
222      // Skip separator row (|---|---|)
223      var dataLines = tableLines.stream().skip(1).filter(l -> !isSeparatorRow(l)).toList();
224
225      // Detect table type: if 2 columns and first header is "Property"/"Key", treat as key/value table
226      var isKeyValue = headers.size() == 2
227         && (headers.get(0).equalsIgnoreCase("Property") || headers.get(0).equalsIgnoreCase("Key"))
228         && headers.get(1).equalsIgnoreCase("Value");
229
230      if (isKeyValue)
231         return parseKeyValueTable(headers, dataLines, eType, outer);
232      return parseMultiColumnTable(headers, dataLines, eType, outer);
233   }
234
235   /**
236    * Parses a 2-column key/value Markdown table.
237    *
238    * @param headers The header names.
239    * @param dataLines The data row lines.
240    * @param eType The expected type.
241    * @param outer The outer object.
242    * @return The parsed bean, map, or other object.
243    * @throws ParseException If parsing fails.
244    */
245   @SuppressWarnings({
246      "java:S135" // Two continue statements needed: skip short rows and skip _type row
247   })
248   protected Object parseKeyValueTable(List<String> headers, List<String> dataLines, ClassMeta<?> eType, Object outer) throws ParseException {
249
250      // For types with ObjectSwaps or that can't be populated via BeanMap property-setting,
251      // convert the key-value table to a JSON5 object and delegate to the full JSON5 parser.
252      if (needsJson5Path(eType)) {
253         var json5 = keyValueTableToJson5(dataLines);
254         try {
255            return getJson5Parser().parseWithOuter(json5, eType, outer);
256         } catch (Exception e) {
257            throw new ParseException(this, e, "Could not parse key-value table as ''{0}'' via JSON5 ''{1}''.", eType, json5);
258         }
259      }
260
261      if (eType.isBean()) {
262         // First pass: look for _type to resolve concrete class for abstract/interface types
263         ClassMeta<?> actualType = eType;
264         for (var line : dataLines) {
265            var cells = splitTableRow(line);
266            if (cells.size() >= 2 && CONST_type.equals(cells.get(0))) {
267               var typeName = cells.get(1);
268               var registry = eType.getBeanRegistry();
269               var resolved = registry != null ? registry.getClassMeta(typeName) : null;
270               if (resolved != null)
271                  actualType = resolved;
272               break;
273            }
274         }
275         var m = newBeanMap(outer, actualType.inner());
276         for (var line : dataLines) {
277            var cells = splitTableRow(line);
278            if (cells.size() < 2)
279               continue;
280            var key = cells.get(0);
281
282            // Skip _type row - it was used for type resolution above
283            if (CONST_type.equals(key))
284               continue;
285
286            var rawVal = cells.get(1);
287            var pm = m.getPropertyMeta(key);
288            if (pm != null) {
289               setCurrentProperty(pm);
290               var pmcm = (ClassMeta<?>) pm.getBeanInfo();
291               var val = parseCellValue(rawVal, pmcm, m.getBean(false));
292               try {
293                  setName(pmcm, val, key);
294               } catch (Exception e) {
295                  throw new ParseException(this, e, "Could not set @NameProperty on ''{0}''.", key);
296               }
297               pm.set(m, key, val);
298               setCurrentProperty(null);
299            } else {
300               onUnknownProperty(key, m, rawVal);
301            }
302         }
303         return m.getBean();
304      }
305
306      if (eType.isMap()) {
307         @SuppressWarnings({
308            "java:S3740" // Raw Map needed for generic map construction from ClassMeta
309         })
310         Map map = eType.canCreateNewInstance(outer) ? (Map) eType.newInstance(outer) : newGenericMap();
311         var keyType = eType.getKeyType() != null ? eType.getKeyType() : string();
312         var valueType = eType.getValueType() != null ? eType.getValueType() : object();
313         for (var line : dataLines) {
314            var cells = splitTableRow(line);
315            if (cells.size() < 2)
316               continue;
317            var rawKey = cells.get(0);
318            var key = parseCellValue(rawKey, keyType, null);
319            // Pass the map being built as the outer when parsing values - non-static inner
320            // classes whose enclosing class is the map type (e.g. H1 inside H extends LinkedHashMap)
321            // need that map instance to satisfy their implicit outer-class constructor argument.
322            var val = parseCellValue(cells.get(1), valueType, map);
323            try {
324               setName(valueType, val, key);
325            } catch (Exception e) {
326               throw new ParseException(this, e, "Could not set @NameProperty for map key ''{0}''.", key);
327            }
328            map.put(key, val);
329         }
330         return map;
331      }
332
333      // Object or unknown: return MarshalledMap, check for _type and cast if possible
334      var resultMap = newGenericMap();
335      for (var line : dataLines) {
336         var cells = splitTableRow(line);
337         if (cells.size() < 2)
338            continue;
339         var key = cells.get(0);
340         var val = parseCellValue(cells.get(1), object(), null);
341         resultMap.put(key, val);
342      }
343      
344      // Check for _type property and cast to typed bean if found
345      if (resultMap.containsKey(getBeanTypePropertyName(eType))) {
346         return cast(resultMap, null, eType);
347      }
348      
349      return resultMap;
350   }
351
352   /**
353    * Parses a multi-column Markdown table (N columns).
354    *
355    * @param headers The column header names.
356    * @param dataLines The data row lines.
357    * @param eType The expected type.
358    * @param outer The outer object.
359    * @return The parsed list of beans, maps, or other objects.
360    * @throws ParseException If parsing fails.
361    */
362   protected Object parseMultiColumnTable(List<String> headers, List<String> dataLines, ClassMeta<?> eType, Object outer) throws ParseException {
363      ClassMeta<?> elementType;
364      Collection<Object> result;
365
366      if (eType.isArray()) {
367         elementType = eType.getElementType();
368         result = new ArrayList<>();
369      } else if (eType.isCollection()) {
370         elementType = eType.getElementType();
371         result = eType.canCreateNewInstance(outer) ? (Collection<Object>) eType.newInstance() : new ArrayList<>();
372      } else if (eType.isObject()) {
373         elementType = object();
374         result = new ArrayList<>();
375      } else {
376         // Single bean/map with multiple columns — not a standard use case, parse first row
377         if (!dataLines.isEmpty()) {
378            var cells = splitTableRow(dataLines.get(0));
379            return parseRow(headers, cells, eType, outer);
380         }
381         return null;
382      }
383
384      for (var line : dataLines) {
385         var cells = splitTableRow(line);
386         var item = parseRow(headers, cells, elementType, result);
387         result.add(item);
388      }
389
390      if (eType.isArray())
391         return toArray(eType, result);
392
393      return result;
394   }
395
396   /**
397    * Parses a single data row into the specified element type.
398    *
399    * @param headers The column header names.
400    * @param cells The cell values for this row.
401    * @param eType The element type.
402    * @param outer The outer object.
403    * @return The parsed element.
404    * @throws ParseException If parsing fails.
405    */
406   protected Object parseRow(List<String> headers, List<String> cells, ClassMeta<?> eType, Object outer) throws ParseException {
407      // Check if all cells are null - if so, return null for the entire row
408      boolean allNull = true;
409      for (var cell : cells) {
410         if (cell != null && !cell.equals(nullValue) && !cell.trim().isEmpty()) {
411            allNull = false;
412            break;
413         }
414      }
415      if (allNull) {
416         return null;
417      }
418
419      // Check if there's a _type column and resolve the actual type using the bean registry
420      ClassMeta<?> actualType = eType;
421      int typeColIndex = headers.indexOf(CONST_type);
422      if (typeColIndex >= 0 && typeColIndex < cells.size()) {
423         var typeName = cells.get(typeColIndex);
424         if (ne(typeName)) {
425            var registry = eType != null ? eType.getBeanRegistry() : null;
426            var resolved = registry != null ? registry.getClassMeta(typeName) : null;
427            if (resolved != null)
428               actualType = resolved;
429         }
430      }
431
432      // For types with ObjectSwaps or that can't be populated via BeanMap property-setting,
433      // convert the row to a JSON5 object and delegate to the full JSON5 parser.  This correctly
434      // handles ObjectSwaps, @ParentProperty, JsonMap-constructor beans, etc.
435      if (needsJson5Path(actualType)) {
436         var json5 = rowToJson5(headers, cells);
437            try {
438            return getJson5Parser().parseWithOuter(json5, actualType, outer);
439         } catch (Exception e) {
440            throw new ParseException(this, e, "Could not parse table row as ''{0}'' via JSON5 ''{1}''.", actualType, json5);
441         }
442      }
443
444      if (actualType == null || actualType.isObject()) {
445         var m = newGenericMap();
446         for (var i = 0; i < headers.size(); i++) {
447            var header = headers.get(i);
448            if (CONST_type.equals(header))
449               continue;
450            var val = i < cells.size() ? cells.get(i) : null;
451            m.put(header, parseCellValue(val, object(), null));
452         }
453         if (typeColIndex >= 0 && m.size() > 0)
454            return cast(m, null, eType);
455         return m;
456      }
457      if (actualType.isBean()) {
458         var m = newBeanMap(outer, actualType.inner());
459         for (var i = 0; i < headers.size(); i++) {
460            var header = headers.get(i);
461            if (CONST_type.equals(header))
462               continue;
463            var rawVal = i < cells.size() ? cells.get(i) : null;
464            var pm = m.getPropertyMeta(header);
465            if (pm != null) {
466               setCurrentProperty(pm);
467               var val = parseCellValue(rawVal, (ClassMeta<?>) pm.getBeanInfo(), m.getBean(false));
468               pm.set(m, header, val);
469               setCurrentProperty(null);
470            } else {
471               onUnknownProperty(header, m, rawVal);
472            }
473         }
474         return m.getBean();
475      }
476      if (actualType.isMap()) {
477         @SuppressWarnings({
478            "java:S3740" // Raw Map needed for generic map construction from ClassMeta
479         })
480         Map map = actualType.canCreateNewInstance(outer) ? (Map) actualType.newInstance(outer) : newGenericMap();
481         var keyType = actualType.getKeyType() != null ? actualType.getKeyType() : string();
482         var valueType = actualType.getValueType() != null ? actualType.getValueType() : object();
483         for (var i = 0; i < headers.size(); i++) {
484            var header = headers.get(i);
485            if (CONST_type.equals(header))
486               continue;
487            var key = convertAttrToType(map, header, keyType);
488            var rawVal = i < cells.size() ? cells.get(i) : null;
489            // Pass the map being built as the outer when parsing values - mirrors the
490            // key-value-table branch so non-static inner classes whose enclosing class is the
491            // map type can be instantiated correctly.
492            var val = parseCellValue(rawVal, valueType, map);
493            map.put(key, val);
494         }
495         return map;
496      }
497      // Simple type: use first cell
498      return parseCellValue(cells.isEmpty() ? null : cells.get(0), actualType, null);
499   }
500
501   /**
502    * Parses a Markdown bulleted list into a collection or array.
503    *
504    * @param lines All input lines.
505    * @param eType The expected type.
506    * @param outer The outer object.
507    * @return The parsed collection, array, or single element.
508    * @throws ParseException If parsing fails.
509    */
510   protected Object parseBulletList(List<String> lines, ClassMeta<?> eType, Object outer) throws ParseException {
511      var items = new ArrayList<String>();
512      for (var line : lines) {
513         var trimmed = line.trim();
514         if (trimmed.startsWith("- ") || trimmed.startsWith("* ") || trimmed.startsWith("+ "))
515            items.add(trimmed.substring(2));
516         else if (trimmed.equals("-") || trimmed.equals("*") || trimmed.equals("+"))
517            items.add("");
518      }
519
520      ClassMeta<?> elementType;
521      Collection<Object> result;
522
523      if (eType.isArray()) {
524         elementType = eType.getElementType();
525         result = new ArrayList<>();
526      } else if (eType.isCollection()) {
527         elementType = eType.getElementType();
528         result = eType.canCreateNewInstance(outer) ? (Collection<Object>) eType.newInstance() : new ArrayList<>();
529      } else {
530         elementType = eType.isObject() ? object() : eType;
531         result = new ArrayList<>();
532      }
533
534      for (var item : items)
535         result.add(parseCellValue(item, elementType, null));
536
537      if (eType.isArray())
538         return toArray(eType, result);
539
540      return result;
541   }
542
543   /**
544    * Returns a Json5Parser that shares this session's bean context (swaps, implClasses, dictionary).
545    *
546    * <p>
547    * Memoized via {@link Memoizer} — first call constructs the parser; subsequent calls return the cached instance.
548    */
549   private JsonParser getJson5Parser() {
550      return json5Parser.get();
551   }
552
553   /**
554    * Converts a raw Markdown table cell string to the target type.
555    *
556    * <p>
557    * If the cell matches the configured null value, returns {@code null}.
558    * If the cell is backtick-wrapped JSON5, parses via {@link Json5Parser}.
559    * Otherwise, attempts numeric/boolean auto-detection when the target type is {@code Object}.
560    *
561    * @param <T> The target type.
562    * @param val The raw cell string (already unescaped).
563    * @param eType The target type.
564    * @param outer The outer object (parent bean) for @ParentProperty support.
565    * @return The parsed value.
566    * @throws ParseException If parsing fails.
567    */
568   protected <T> T parseCellValue(String val, ClassMeta<T> eType, Object outer) throws ParseException {
569      if (val == null)
570         return null;
571      val = val.trim();
572      if (nullValue != null && val.equals(nullValue))
573         return null;
574      if (isTrimStrings())
575         val = val.trim();
576      // For String types, preserve empty cells as empty strings; for other types, treat as null
577      if (val.isEmpty())
578         return eType.isString() ? (T) "" : null;
579
580      // Inline JSON5 wrapped in backticks — use context-aware parser with outer for full support
581      // (handles ObjectSwaps, @ParentProperty, JsonMap constructors, etc.)
582      if (val.startsWith("`") && val.endsWith("`") && val.length() > 1) {
583         var inner = val.substring(1, val.length() - 1);
584         try {
585            return getJson5Parser().parseWithOuter(inner, eType, outer);
586         } catch (Exception e) {
587            throw new ParseException(this, e, "Could not parse inline JSON5 ''{0}'' to type ''{1}''.", inner, eType);
588         }
589      }
590
591      // Unescape table cell escaping
592      val = unescapeCell(val);
593
594      if (eType.isObject()) {
595         // Auto-detect type
596         if ("true".equals(val)) return (T) Boolean.TRUE;
597         if ("false".equals(val)) return (T) Boolean.FALSE;
598         try { return (T) Integer.valueOf(val); } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException ignored) { /* not int, try next */ }
599         try { return (T) Long.valueOf(val); } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException ignored) { /* not long, try next */ }
600         try { return (T) Double.valueOf(val); } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException ignored) { /* not double, treat as string */ }
601         return (T) val;
602      }
603
604      // Date / time / duration cluster: route through the format-aware parsers so the configured
605      // MarshallingContext.get<Format>() hint reaches the wire-form coercion.  Without this the
606      // fall-through to convertToType(val, eType) below would route a bare numeric cell through a
607      // generic Number → T coercion (e.g. Duration.ofMillis(long)) that drops the format hint.
608      if (eType.isDate())
609         return parseDate(val, eType);
610      if (eType.isCalendar())
611         return parseCalendar(val, eType);
612      if (eType.isTemporal())
613         return parseTemporal(val, eType);
614      if (eType.isDuration())
615         return (T) parseDuration(val);
616      if (eType.isPeriod())
617         return (T) parsePeriod(val);
618
619      try {
620         return convertToType(val, eType);
621      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") Exception e) {
622         throw new ParseException(this, "Could not convert Markdown cell value ''{0}'' to type ''{1}''.", val, eType);
623      }
624   }
625
626   /**
627    * Returns true if the given ClassMeta requires the full JSON5 parsing machinery to be correctly
628    * instantiated — i.e., it has an ObjectSwap, cannot be created as a standard bean, or cannot be
629    * populated via direct property-setting through a BeanMap.
630    *
631    * <p>
632    * When this returns true, table rows and key-value tables should be converted to a JSON5 string
633    * and parsed via {@link Json5Parser} (using
634    * {@link Parser#parseWithOuter}) to correctly handle swaps,
635    * {@link ParentProperty @ParentProperty}, and non-standard constructors.
636    */
637   private boolean needsJson5Path(ClassMeta<?> type) {
638      if (type == null || type.isObject() || type.isMap() || type.isPrimitive() || type.isNumber() || type.isString())
639         return false;
640      // If the type has an ObjectSwap, it cannot be parsed via BeanMap property-setting
641      if (type.getSwap(this) != null)
642         return true;
643      // If not a bean (no public properties), can't use BeanMap approach
644      return !type.isBean();
645   }
646
647   /**
648    * Converts a single Markdown table cell value to its JSON5 literal representation.
649    *
650    * <p>
651    * Inline JSON5 (backtick-wrapped) is unwrapped directly. Booleans and numbers are written bare.
652    * Everything else is single-quoted as a JSON5 string.
653    */
654   private String cellToJson5(String cell) {
655      if (cell == null || cell.equals(nullValue))
656         return "null";
657      cell = cell.trim();
658      if (cell.isEmpty())
659         return "''";
660      // Inline JSON5 wrapped in backticks — unwrap and embed directly
661      if (cell.startsWith("`") && cell.endsWith("`") && cell.length() > 1)
662         return cell.substring(1, cell.length() - 1);
663      // Unescape Markdown table escaping first
664      cell = unescapeCell(cell);
665      // Booleans and numbers don't need quoting
666      if ("true".equals(cell) || "false".equals(cell))
667         return cell;
668      try { Integer.parseInt(cell); return cell; } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException ignored) { /* not int, try next */ }
669      try { Long.parseLong(cell); return cell; } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException ignored) { /* not long, try next */ }
670      try { Double.parseDouble(cell); return cell; } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException ignored) { /* not double, quote as string */ }
671      // String: single-quote with escaping for JSON5
672      return "'" + cell.replace("\\", "\\\\").replace("'", "\\'") + "'";
673   }
674
675   /**
676    * Converts a table row (headers + cells) into a JSON5 object string, suitable for parsing via
677    * {@link Json5Parser}.
678    *
679    * <p>
680    * Example: headers=["f1","f2"], cells=["a","2"] → <code>{f1:'a',f2:2}</code>
681    *
682    * <p>
683    * The {@code _type} column, if present, is included as-is (as a quoted string) so that the JSON5
684    * parser can perform type resolution via the bean dictionary.
685    */
686   private String rowToJson5(List<String> headers, List<String> cells) {
687      var sb = new StringBuilder("{");
688      var first = true;
689      for (var i = 0; i < headers.size(); i++) {
690         var header = headers.get(i);
691         var cell = i < cells.size() ? cells.get(i) : null;
692         if (!first) sb.append(",");
693         first = false;
694         sb.append(header).append(":");
695         if (CONST_type.equals(header)) {
696            // Keep _type as a quoted string so the JSON5 parser can resolve it
697            sb.append(cell == null ? "null" : "'" + cell + "'");
698         } else {
699            sb.append(cellToJson5(cell));
700         }
701      }
702      sb.append("}");
703      return sb.toString();
704   }
705
706   /**
707    * Converts a key-value table (list of [key, value] row lines) into a JSON5 object string.
708    */
709   private String keyValueTableToJson5(List<String> dataLines) {
710      var sb = new StringBuilder("{");
711      var first = true;
712      for (var line : dataLines) {
713         var cells = splitTableRow(line);
714         if (cells.size() < 2)
715            continue;
716         var key = cells.get(0);
717         var val = cells.get(1);
718         if (!first) sb.append(",");
719         first = false;
720         sb.append(key).append(":");
721         if (CONST_type.equals(key)) {
722            sb.append(val == null ? "null" : "'" + val + "'");
723         } else {
724            sb.append(cellToJson5(val));
725         }
726      }
727      sb.append("}");
728      return sb.toString();
729   }
730
731   /**
732    * Splits a Markdown table row into its cell values, stripping leading/trailing whitespace and pipes.
733    *
734    * @param line The raw table row line.
735    * @return The list of trimmed cell values.
736    */
737   protected List<String> splitTableRow(String line) {
738      var trimmed = line.trim();
739      if (trimmed.startsWith("|"))
740         trimmed = trimmed.substring(1);
741      if (trimmed.endsWith("|") && (trimmed.length() < 2 || trimmed.charAt(trimmed.length() - 2) != '\\'))
742         trimmed = trimmed.substring(0, trimmed.length() - 1);
743
744      // Split on unescaped pipe characters
745      var result = new ArrayList<String>();
746      var sb = new StringBuilder();
747      var i = 0;
748      while (i < trimmed.length()) {
749         var c = trimmed.charAt(i);
750         if (c == '\\' && i + 1 < trimmed.length()) {
751            var next = trimmed.charAt(i + 1);
752            if (next == '|' || next == '\\') {
753               sb.append('\\');
754               sb.append(next);
755               i += 2;
756               continue;
757            }
758         }
759         if (c == '|') {
760            result.add(sb.toString().trim());
761            sb.setLength(0);
762         } else {
763            sb.append(c);
764         }
765         i++;
766      }
767      if (!sb.isEmpty() || !result.isEmpty())
768         result.add(sb.toString().trim());
769      return result;
770   }
771
772   /**
773    * Returns whether a table row is a separator row (e.g. {@code |---|---|}).
774    *
775    * @param line The table row line.
776    * @return Whether it is a separator row.
777    */
778   protected boolean isSeparatorRow(String line) {
779      var trimmed = line.trim();
780      if (!trimmed.startsWith("|"))
781         return false;
782      // Equivalent of trimmed.replaceAll("[|\\-: ]", "").isEmpty(): true iff every char is one of | - : space.
783      for (var i = 0; i < trimmed.length(); i++) {
784         var c = trimmed.charAt(i);
785         if (c != '|' && c != '-' && c != ':' && c != ' ')
786            return false;
787      }
788      return true;
789   }
790
791   /**
792    * Reads all lines from the given reader.
793    *
794    * @param r The reader.
795    * @return All lines.
796    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
797    */
798   protected List<String> readAllLines(java.io.BufferedReader r) throws IOException {
799      var lines = new ArrayList<String>();
800      String line;
801      while ((line = r.readLine()) != null)
802         lines.add(line);
803      return lines;
804   }
805
806   /**
807    * Unescapes Markdown table cell escape sequences.
808    *
809    * @param s The escaped cell value.
810    * @return The unescaped value.
811    */
812   protected String unescapeCell(String s) {
813      if (s == null || !s.contains("\\"))
814         return s;
815      var sb = new StringBuilder(s.length());
816      var i = 0;
817      while (i < s.length()) {
818         var c = s.charAt(i);
819         if (c == '\\' && i + 1 < s.length()) {
820            var next = s.charAt(i + 1);
821            if (next == '|' || next == '\\') {
822               sb.append(next);
823               i += 2;
824               continue;
825            }
826         }
827         sb.append(c);
828         i++;
829      }
830      return sb.toString();
831   }
832}
